section.name: JSON Schema and Catalog Artifact Index
section.t: The following JSON Schema definitions are normatively referenced throughout this specification. They are part of the archived publication package for this draft in JSON Schema Draft 2020-12 format under schemas/. The Draft-02 AIP Catalog Bundle is maintained as the deterministic dist/catalog.json release artifact in the separate aip-catalog repository. The working repository at aip-rfc is a convenience mirror; a branch head, mutable tag, or /latest schema URI is not a normative artifact.

For this draft, the immutable schema identifier for each listed schema is the $id value ending in /draft-02. Implementations claiming Draft-02 conformance MUST validate against those /draft-02 identifiers. If a local filename, repository URL, or mirror URL conflicts with the schema identified by the $id, the $id and the archived publication package are authoritative.

dl.dd: Defines the Agent Identity Object structure (Section 5.2).
Defines the default GET /v1/agents/{aid} Agent Registration Metadata response ().
Defines the Registry Metadata returned by GET /v1/registry-metadata ().
Defines the AIP gateway OAuth Protected Resource Metadata returned by GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource ().
Defines the Public-Key response returned by GET /v1/agents/{aid}/public-key and GET /v1/agents/{aid}/public-key/{key-id} ().
Defines the Capability Manifest structure (Section 5.3).
Defines the Credential Token JWT payload structure (Section 5.4).
Defines the Step Execution Token JWT payload structure (Sections 5.4.1 and 13.8).
Defines the Principal Token JWT payload structure (Section 5.5).
Defines the Registration Envelope request body structure (Section 5.6).
Defines the Registered Resource Record used by the Registry resource registration endpoints ().
Defines the Registry Trust Record structure used for Registry trust bootstrapping and continuity (Section 7.3).
Defines the Revocation Object structure (Section 5.7).
Defines the live Revocation Status response for GET /v1/agents/{aid}/revocation ().
Defines the signed AIP CRL document returned by GET /v1/crl and signed endpoints.crl distribution URIs ( and ).
Defines the Endorsement Object structure (Section 5.8).
Defines the standard AIP JSON error response body ().
Defines the Approval Envelope, Step, and Compensation Step structures (Section 13).
Defines request and response body profiles for Approval Envelope forward-step and compensation-step claim, complete, and fail endpoints ( and ).
Defines the Capability Overlay structure (Section 5.11).
Defines the Engagement Object structure including Participant, Approval Gate, and Change Log Entry (Section 5.12).
Defines the Grant Request structure (Section 12.2).
Defines the Grant Response structure (Section 12.4).
Defines Registry Push Notification Protocol subscription request, subscription response/status, and push payload structures ().

section.t: All schema files conform to JSON Schema Draft 2020-12. Implementations MUST validate objects against these schemas. Validation failures MUST result in rejection.

The Draft-02 AIP Catalog Bundle is the immutable catalog artifact identified by catalog_snapshot_id: "https://provai.dev/aip/catalog/draft-02". Its authoritative bytes are the bytes of the immutable dist/catalog.json release artifact in the provai-dev/aip-catalog repository, or a mirror whose SHA-256 digest exactly matches the aip_catalog_sha256 value published by the Registry. Catalog retrieval, mirroring, and digest validation rules are defined in . A Registry MUST NOT use a mutable catalog source to determine Draft-02 conformance.
